title: todo_write validation builds entire Vec before locking — non-atomic with concurrent writers
description:
## Severity
Minor

## Dimension
Concurrency / Functional Correctness

## Location
src/tools/todo.rs:76-162 (execute_todo_write)

## Violation
The function does ALL validation first (lines 85-128, building new_todos: Vec<TodoItem>), then takes the Mutex (line 153) to write. The intent is "validate before mutating" — fine. But the comment at line 139 says "Claude Code parity: when every item is completed, clear the list" and that decision is computed BEFORE the lock is taken — based on the new input only. If two threads concurrently call todo_write for the same session:

- Thread A computes `all_done = true` from its inputs and races to remove the bucket.
- Thread B computes `all_done = false` from its inputs and races to insert.

The order of map.remove vs map.insert is non-deterministic; A's "all done" can erase B's pending tasks (or vice versa). The validation-before-lock pattern is fine, but the lock should hold the "decide what to do AND do it" pair atomically.

Also: `new_todos.clone()` (line 147) is unnecessary work — `stored_todos` only needs to exist OR not. Use `mem::take` or restructure as an Option<Vec<_>>.

## Mandated refactor
1. Move the all_done decision INSIDE the locked block so the "compute and write" is one critical section.
2. Replace `let stored_todos = if all_done { Vec::new() } else { new_todos.clone() };` with `let stored_todos: Option<Vec<TodoItem>> = if all_done { None } else { Some(new_todos.clone()) };` and use match inside the lock.
3. Better: use a transactional Mutex helper like `map.entry(key).and_modify(...).or_insert_with(...)` to keep the entire decision/write inside one critical section.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
